The '''continuative aspect''' (also called '''continuous''') in Aelaki expresses an ongoing, imperfective action. It is formed through '''C₂ reduplication''' of the verb stem.

== Formation ==

The continuative is formed by reduplicating the second root consonant (C₂) along with its surrounding vowels.

Using the root ''zh-r-n'' "live" (base form: ''zharan''):

{| class="wikitable"
! Aspect !! Form !! Pattern !! Meaning
|-
| Base || zharan || C₁V₁C₂V₂C₃ || (someone) lives
|-
| Continuative || zhororon || C₂ reduplication || living (in progress)
|}

== Behavior across tenses ==

Unlike the other aspects, the continuative does not change its function between past and present tense. It consistently expresses an ongoing, in-progress action regardless of temporal context.

{| class="wikitable"
! Past aspect !! Present realization
|-
| [[Atelic|Atelic perfect]] || [[Habitual]]
|-
| '''Continuative''' || '''Continuative''' (unchanged)
|-
| [[Telic|Telic perfect]] || [[Gnomic]]
|}

== Stative verb continuatives ==

[[Stative verbs]] form their continuative by reduplicating the aspectual prefix rather than C₂. Examples with ''zoduk'' "know":

{| class="wikitable"
! Stative aspect !! Base !! Continuative !! Meaning
|-
| [[Inchoative]] || nüzoduk || nünüzoduk || learning
|-
| [[Cessative]] || nïzedik || nïnïzedik || forgetting
|-
| [[Resumptive]] || luzedik || luluzedik || remembering
|-
| [[Almost]] || kozoduk || kokozoduk || almost learning
|}

== See also ==

* [[Imperfect]]
* [[Habitual]]
* [[Gnomic]]

[[Category:Continuities?]]
[[Category:git synced pages]]
